options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: use_advanced_characters
options.description:
Whether to use the advanced characters feature. The normal options for character, ascension, etc. are ignored.
See the "advanced_characters" option.

options.name: advanced_characters
options.description:
The configuration for advanced characters.  Each character's options can be configured
independently of each other.  No validation is done on the character name, so use carefully.
Format is:
    <char name>:
        ascension: <number>
        ascension_down: <number>

If using a modded character:
Enter the internal ID of the character to use.

 if you don't know the exact ID to enter with the mod installed go to
`Mods -> Archipelago Multi-world -> config` to view a list of installed modded character IDs.

If the chosen character mod is not installed, checks will be sent when another character
sends them.  If none of the chosen character mods are installed, you will be playing
a very boring Ironclad run.

options.name: ascension
options.description: What Ascension do you wish to play with. Note that logic is written assuming ascension 1

options.name: ascension_down
options.description:
The number of ascension downs to add to the item pool, per character. Only valid when
`use_advanced_characters` is false (see `advanced_characters`), and when `include_floor_checks` is true.
Will be ignored if invalid.

Logic does NOT account for this.

options.name: filler_weights
options.description:
The list of filler and corresponding weights that will be added to the item pool
1 Gold - One gold, character bound
5 Gold - Five gold, character bound
CAW CAW - CAW CAW

options.name: shop_sanity
options.description: Whether to shuffle shop slots into the pool.  Also adds new locations at the shop per slot shuffled.

options.name: shop_card_slots
options.description: When shop_sanity is enabled, the number of colored card slots to shuffle.

options.name: shop_neutral_card_slots
options.description: When shop_sanity is enabled, the number of neutral card slots to shuffle.

options.name: shop_relic_slots
options.description: When shop_sanity is enabled, the number of relic slots to shuffle.

options.name: shop_potion_slots
options.description: When shop_sanity is enabled, the number of potion slots to shuffle

options.name: shop_remove_slots
options.description:
When shop_sanity is enabled, whether to shuffle the ability to remove cards at the shop.
Progressive based on Act; i.e. you'll gain the ability to remove cards per Act, starting from Act 1.
Act 4 will be treated as Act 3.

options.name: shop_sanity_costs
options.description:
How expensive the AP shop items should be. Tiered means costs map to typical costs rarity for the slot.
Progression = Rare, Useful = Uncommon, Filler = Common
Logic does not take this option into account.
Fixed=15 gold each
Super_Discount_Tiered=20% of tiered costs
Discount_Tiered=50% of tiered costs
Tiered=Vanilla price for slot

options.name: seeded
options.description: Whether each character should have a fixed seed to climb the spire with or not.

options.name: characters
options.description:
Enter the list of characters to play as.  Valid characters are:
'Ironclad'
'Silent'
'Defect'
'Regent'
'Necrobinder'

options.name: pick_num_characters
options.description:
Randomly select from the configured characters this many characters to generate for.
0 disables.
For example, if "character" is configured to be:
    character:
        - Ironclad
        - Silent
        - Defect
And pick_num_characters is set to 2, then one possible outcome is
to have a run with Ironclad and Defect, but not the Silent.

options.name: num_chars_goal
options.description: How many characters you need to complete a run with before you goal. 0 means all characters

options.name: lock_characters
options.description:
Whether in a multi character run "Unlock [Char]" items should be shuffled in.
locked_fixed means the unlocked_character option is used to determine which character to start with
locked_random means which character you start with is randomized
unlocked means you start with all characters available

options.name: unlocked_character
options.description:
Which character to start unlocked, if lock_characters is set to locked_fixed.
Can also enter a character name for modded characters.

options.name: shuffle_all_cards
options.description: Whether every card reward is shuffled.  If false, then every other card reward is shuffled

options.name: include_floor_checks
options.description: Whether to include reaching new floors as a location.  Adds small amounts of gold as items.

options.name: campfire_sanity
options.description:
Whether to shuffle being able to rest and smith at each campsite per act.  Also adds
new locations at campsites per act.

options.name: gold_sanity
options.description: Whether to enable shuffling gold rewards into the multiworld. Adds 22 locations per character

options.name: potion_sanity
options.description: Whether to enable shuffling potion drops into the multiworld; adds 9 locations per character.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: enable_death_fragments
options.description:
If Death Link is enabled, turning this setting on gives you a Curse Card whenever
you receive a Death Link.

options.name: death_link_damage_percent
options.description:
If Death Link is enabled, this setting determines how much damage you take when you receive a Death Link, 
as a percentage of your max health. 
If you do not want to take any damage, set this to 0. 
If you want to be killed whenever you receive a Death Link, set this to 100.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: exclude_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.
